question: Which Operate capability is described in the introduction as moving many active process instances at once so execution can continue?

options.text: Process instance modification, which is performed per single active instance.
Batch move of active process instances, which the introduction lists as letting execution continue on many instances at once.
Delete finished instances, which targets instances that have already terminated.
Resolve incident, which automatically advances tokens past the failing element.

a.text: The introduction describes process instance modification as a way to keep one active instance going, not as a bulk move across many instances.

b.text: The introduction explicitly lists 'Batch move active process instances to allow execution to continue' as a distinct Operate capability.

c.text: Deleting finished instances acts on completed instances; it does not move active ones to a new state.

d.text: Resolving an incident addresses a failure; it does not, by itself, move tokens of many instances to allow execution to continue.

explanation: Operate distinguishes single-instance modification from batch move: modification fixes one active instance, while batch move applies a move across many active instances at once so they can continue execution.
